events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: unsealed duct joints at rough-complete stage. IMC 603 requires sealed duct joints. SMACNA requires sealing. Cloth duct tape is not acceptable. This is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: medium-high (leakage, energy, IAQ). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete ~85. Confidence high.

"findings": [
"Unsealed duct joints observed at rough-complete stage; no mastic or approved foil tape applied.",
"Cloth duct tape is not an approved duct sealant and indicates non-compliant sealing method.",
"Unsealed joints will cause excessive leakage, reduced system performance, and non-compliance with IMC 603 and SMACNA sealing requirements."
],
"actions": [
"Remove any cloth duct tape and clean joint surfaces.",
"Seal all transverse joints, longitudinal seams, and branch connections with code-approved mastic or listed foil tape per SMACNA and IMC 603.",
"Re-inspect after sealing and before duct closure/insulation.",
"Perform leakage testing if required by spec."
],
"rationale": "IMC 603 and SMACNA require duct joints and seams to be securely fastened and sealed with approved materials. Unsealed joints, and especially cloth duct tape, do not meet code or industry standards. At rough-complete the sealing should already be done, so the work is non-compliant and must be corrected before closeout.",
"references": ["IMC 603", "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards", "ACCA Manual D"]
}
